Title: An Allele of Glutamate Formiminotransferase Triggers 5-Methyl-Tetrahydrofolate-to-MeFox Conversion and Facilitates Folate Biofortification in Maize

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: 5-M-THF is the major bioactive folate vitamer and one-carbon donor relevant to human folate nutrition; MeFox is an oxidation-derived folate-related product of unknown biological function
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glutamate formiminotransferase-like protein; single formiminotransferase domain; folic-acid-binding protein
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Triggers conversion of 5-M-THF to MeFox; loss of function increases 5-M-THF and decreases MeFox, while overexpression shifts folate metabolism toward MeFox
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS mapped 5-M-THF variation to ZmGFT/GRMZM2G124863. 2. CRISPR loss of ZmGFT increased seed 5-M-THF about 3.3-fold and reduced MeFox, demonstrating that ZmGFT normally channels 5-M-THF toward MeFox. 3. Overexpression of ZmGFT, especially the G-allele from Qi319, increased MeFox and altered folate profiles. 4. In vitro assays showed recombinant ZmGFT converts 5-M-THF to MeFox, revealing a new plant folate fate relevant to biofortification.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in maize seed endosperm at 15-25 DAP; also detected in roots, stems, leaves, tassels, embryos/endosperm
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in endosperm at 15-25 DAP in maize
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown association panels in Hainan, Yunnan, Hubei, and Hebei; young seed developmental stage 20-25 DAP; in vitro incubation with 5-M-THF
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize ubiquitin promoter for overexpression; CaMV 35S promoter in Arabidopsis heterologous expression
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; candidate gene resequencing/association; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; transgenic overexpression; folate profiling by HPLC/MS; recombinant protein assay; docking and molecular dynamics
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent target for folate biofortification; knockout/editing can raise bioactive folate 5-M-THF, while allele selection at S1789 can be used as a molecular marker to breed high-folate sweetcorn and maize
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Slightly shorter plant and ear height in mutants; higher seed protein content; some amino acid changes; little major morphological penalty reported
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite annotation similarity to mammalian formiminotransferase, plant ZmGFT lacks classical formiminotransferase activity and instead catalyzes 5-M-THF-to-MeFox conversion; authors propose alternative name MeFox synthase
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq for GWAS genotyping (~1.03 million SNPs); transcriptome RNA-seq of DAP25 kernels; GenBank KT727273-KT727912, KT727913, KT727914

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive folate nutrition and one-carbon metabolism
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant GFT ortholog with formiminotransferase-like domain
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockdown increases 5-M-THF and strongly decreases MeFox in rice seeds, supporting conserved GFT-mediated 5-M-THF-to-MeFox conversion
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RNAi knockdown of OsGFT in rice caused about 3.4-fold increase in 5-M-THF. 2. MeFox decreased by about 88% in mature seeds. 3. Results support conservation of GFT function in cereal folate metabolism.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Mature seed analysis in transgenic RNAi plants
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize ubiquitin promoter driving RNAi construct
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports GFT suppression/editing as a cereal biofortification strategy
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Subtle decrease in amylose and plant height; no significant difference in effective panicle number or protein content
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Rice validation was used to demonstrate functional conservation rather than discover a separate pathway

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive folate metabolism and one-carbon metabolism
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant GFT ortholog with formiminotransferase-like domain
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Included as conserved ortholog context; plant GFT family conserves 5-M-THF-to-MeFox activity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis GFT was included in phylogenetic and comparative analyses as a plant ortholog. 2. The paper's experimental Arabidopsis validation used maize ZmGFT overexpression rather than direct manipulation of AtGFT.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a primary experimentally manipulated core gene in Results; included only because functional conservation across plants was discussed
